ABOUT.title: About Me
ABOUT.full_about: Senior software engineer with a frontend focus, based in Japan. Outside of high-velocity feature work, I specialize in the work that makes teams faster and more reliable — observability, developer tooling, CI/CD, and AI-assisted workflows. Comfortable working across the stack when the problem calls for it. Bilingual in English and Japanese (JLPT N1).
ABOUT.mark_meta:
Senior Software Engineer
Frontend · CI/CD · AI Tooling
Japan · Remote

EXPERIENCES.title: Experiences
EXPERIENCES.scroll_hint: Scroll down to step through each role

interac.name: Assistant Language Teacher
interac.description: As an ALT (Assistant Language Teacher) at Interac, I taught English across public schools in Fukushima Prefecture. This role was the foundation of my life in Japan and the beginning of the journey that led to JLPT N1.

rapptr.name: Frontend Engineer
rapptr.quote: From app strategy, design and development to launch and ongoing tailored support, we are with you every step of the way – continuously refining and evolving your product.
rapptr.description:
Product agency building web and mobile products for early-stage and growth-stage startups.

- Delivered production systems across multiple client products, collaborating directly with startup founders and engineering teams to scope features, align on technical requirements, and ship to production timelines.

- Built a Figma-synchronized design system of 20+ primitives adopted across client teams — cutting feature scaffolding time by ~50% and eliminating recurring design-development drift on every new product.

- Architected and shipped an embeddable third-party widget from zero, owning the full technical design, cross-origin isolation strategy, and production rollout. The widget became a core platform offering and opened a new client integration channel.

sellfire.name: Senior Software Engineer
sellfire.quote: Sellfire is an all-in-one sales platform for inside sales teams. It offers all the necessary features in one software, including dial functions, real-time reporting, coaching and development, pipeline management, and automated lead management. Sellfire supports team scaling while improving sales performance. With real-time call coaching and report display, it quickly identifies and improves sales opportunities. By using Sellfire, sales teams can operate efficiently and effectively, accelerating business growth.
sellfire.description:
Sole senior engineer, 10-repo multi-product codebase.

- Built the team's Claude Code workflow from scratch. Authored production skills used across engineering — create-jira-ticket (turn any Sentry, PagerDuty, Slack URL, or text into a fully cross-linked Jira ticket with sprint assignment), csp-add (auto-allowlists CDN domains), add-test-ids (adds Playwright-friendly test IDs), and co-authored a weekly reliability review skill that consolidates data from New Relic, PagerDuty, Sentry, and Jira into a single Notion page.

- Took app.sellfire.com's MDN Observatory security score from 25/100 (D−) to 110/100 (A+). Shipped a strict Content Security Policy and modern security headers (HSTS, Permissions-Policy, Referrer-Policy). Built a detection layer that captures violations in Sentry, embeds a runnable csp-add Claude command directly in the alert, and pages the on-call engineer — turning each fix into a copy-paste.

- Built observability from zero. Almost every production issue was customer-reported. Designed and shipped a Sentry + PagerDuty detection and routing layer that cut mean time to resolution (MTTR) from days/weeks to minutes, transferring ownership of many production issues from customers to engineering.

- Led React 16 → 18 migration including updating six core libraries. Eliminated legacy build tooling and cut the main JavaScript bundle by ~50 kB (gzipped) through barrel-import cleanup and removing unused dependencies, measurably reducing page load times. Received unsolicited customer feedback to match: "It's much faster. No lag at all."

- Replaced weekly manual QA test runs with automated feedback in under 15 minutes. Architected CI/CD quality gates using GitHub Actions and Playwright E2E triggered on every staging deployment, catching regressions before they reached production.

AI_FLASHCARDS.name: Infinite Flashcards
AI_FLASHCARDS.description:
This project is a Python script that generates Anki flashcards from a word list using OpenAI. 
 The generated cards include word readings, example sentences, and basic definitions. 
 This Python script significantly simplifies the process of creating new cards, allowing learners to focus on studying rather than card creation.
AI_FLASHCARDS.shortDescription: Generate Anki flashcards from a word list or essay using OpenAI.
